content: The circuit shown in the figure with the switch S open, is in steady state. After the switch S is closed, the time constant of the circuit in seconds is
In the circuit shown below, the switch S is closed at t = 0. The magnitude of the steady state voltage, in volts, across the 6 $$\Omega$$ resistor is ________. (round off to two decimal places).
explanation:
In steady state capactiro acts as an open circuit for DC supply.

The voltage across 6 $$\Omega$$ is

$${V_o} = 10 \times {2 \over {2 + 2}} = 5\,V$$

content: In the circuit, switch 'S' is in the closed position for a very long time. If the switch is opened at time t = 0, then iL (t) in amperes, for t ≥ 0 is
explanation:
The current flowing through inductor before the switch is opened is given by

$$ i\left(0^{-}\right)=\frac{10}{1}=10 \mathrm{~A}=i_L\left(0^{+}\right) $$

(ii) For $\boldsymbol{t}>\mathbf{0}$,

The circuit diagram after the switch is opened is given by

$$ \text { (iii) For } t=\infty \text {, } $$

$$ \text { The current flowing through inductor in steady state is given by } $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & i_L(\infty)=\frac{30+10}{5}=\frac{40}{5}=8 \\ & R_{e q}=5 \Omega \text { and } L=0.5 \mathrm{H} \end{aligned} $$

Time constant of the above circuit is given by,

$$ \tau=\frac{L}{R}=0.1 \mathrm{sec} $$

The charging equation of first order RL circuit is given by,

$$ \begin{aligned} & i_L(t)=i(\infty)+\left[i_L\left(0^{+}\right)-i_L(\infty)\right] e^{-t / \tau} \\ & i_L(t)=i(\infty)+\left[i_L\left(0^{+}\right)-i_L(\infty)\right] e^{-R t / L} \\ & i_L(t)=8+(10-8) e^{-10 t} \\ & i_L(t)=8+2 e^{-10 t} \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the correct option is (A).

content: In the series $$RC$$ circuit shown in Fig. the voltage across $$C$$ starts increasing when the $$d.c.$$ source is switched on. The rate of increase of voltage across $$C$$ at the instant just after the switch is closed (i.e., at $$t = {0^ + }$$), will be
The time constant of the network shown in Fig. is
In the circuit of Fig, the switch $$'S'$$ is closed at $$t=0$$ with $${i_L}\left( 0 \right) = 0$$ and $${V_C}\left( 0 \right) = 0.$$ In the steady state, $${V_C}$$ equals
The switch ( S ) closes at $t=0 \mathrm{sec}$. The time, in sec the capacitor takes to charge to 50 V is __________ . (Round off to one decimal places)
explanation:
Given circuit is shown below,

(i) At $t<0 / t=0^{-} /$steady state : switch is open and capacitor behaves as open circuit, under steady state condition.

The current 3 A is flowing through $25 \Omega$ resistor.

Using ohm's law, $V=25 \times 3=75 \mathrm{~V}$

Apply kVL,

$$ \begin{aligned} & -100+V_c\left(0^{-}\right)+V+2 \times 0=0 \\ & -100+V_c\left(0^{-}\right)+75=0 \\ & V_c\left(0^{-}\right)=25 \mathrm{~V} \end{aligned} $$

From the property of capacitor in transient,

$$ V_c\left(0^{-}\right)=V_c\left(0^{+}\right)=25 \mathrm{~V} $$

(ii) At $t=\infty$ /steady state : switch is closed and capacitor behaves open circuit under steady state condition.

All current of 3 A is flowing through switch (s) hence, current through $25 \Omega$ resistor is 0 .

$$ V=25 \times 0=0 \mathrm{~V} $$

Apply KVL,

$$ \begin{aligned} & -100+V_c(\infty)+2 \times 0=0 \\ & V_c(\infty)=100 \mathrm{~V} \end{aligned} $$

(iii) At $t \geq 0$ (transient) :

For RC network,

Time constant, $\tau=R_{T h} C$

Where, $R_{p_k}=$ Thevenin resistance across capacitor when independent voltage source is short circuited and independent current source is open circuited.

$$ \begin{aligned} & R_{T H}=2 \Omega \\ & \tau=R_{T h} C=2 \times 5=10 \mathrm{sec} \end{aligned} $$

The voltage through capacitor in first order RC circuit is given by,

$$ V_c(t)=V_c(\infty)+\left[V_c\left(0^{+}\right)-V_c(\infty)\right] e^{-t / \tau} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & V_c(t)=100+[25-100] e^{-t / 10} \\ & V_c(t)=100-75 e^{-t / 10} \end{aligned} $$

The time at which capacitor charge upto 50 V is,

$$ \begin{aligned} & 50=100-75 e^{-t / 10} \\ & -50=-75 e^{-t / 10} \\ & \frac{50}{75}=e^{-t / 10} \end{aligned} $$

Taking $\log$ both sides.

$$ \begin{aligned} & \ln \left(\frac{50}{75}\right)=\ln e^{-t / 10} \\ & -0.405=\frac{-t}{10} \\ & t=4.05 \mathrm{sec} \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the correct answer is 4 .

content: The circuit shown in the figure is initially in the steady state with the switch K in open condition and $$\overline K $$ in closed condition. The switch K is closed and $$\overline K $$ is opened simultaneously at the instant $$t=t_1$$, where $$t_1 > 0$$. The minimum value of $$t_1$$ in milliseconds, such that there is no transient in the voltage across the 100 $$\mu$$F capacitor, is ___________ (Round off to 2 decimal places).
explanation:
Given circuit is as shown below,

Given that $K$ is opened and $\bar{K}$ is closed initially. So given circuit becomes,

Figure (A) can be drawn in phase form as shown below,

$$ \begin{aligned} & \therefore I_C=\frac{1 \angle 0^0 \times 10}{10-j 10}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \angle 45^0 \\ & V_C=-j 10 \times I_C=-j 10 \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \angle 45^{\circ}=\frac{10}{\sqrt{2}} \angle-45^{\circ} \\ & \therefore V_C(t)=\frac{10}{\sqrt{2}} \sin \left(1000 t-45^{\circ}\right) \mathrm{V} \end{aligned} $$

Now, at $t=t_1, K$ is closed and $\bar{K}$ is opened. So, the circuit becomes,

$\therefore \quad$ Steady state voltage across capacitor will be, $V_C(\infty)=5$. Now, for transient free condition at $t=t_1$.

$$ \begin{aligned} & V_C\left(t_1\right)=V_C(\infty) \\\\ & \frac{10}{\sqrt{2}} \sin \left(1000 t_1-45^0\right)=5 \\\\ & \sin \left(1000 t_1-45^{\circ}\right)=\frac{5 \sqrt{2}}{10}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \\\\ & 1000 t_1-45^{\circ}=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right) \\\\ & 1000 t_1-45^0=45^{\circ} \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & 1000 t_1=90^{\circ}=\frac{\pi}{2} \\\\ & t_1=\frac{\pi}{2} \mathrm{~ms}=1.57 \mathrm{~ms} \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the correct answer is 1.57 ms .

content:
A 100 Hz square wave, switching between 0 V and 5 V, is applied to a CR high-pass filter circuit as shown. The output voltage waveform across the resistor is 6.2 V peak-to-peak. If the resistance R is 820 Ω, then the value C is ______ μF.

(Round off to 2 decimal places)
explanation:
Given :

(i) Square waveform of 0 to 5 V amplitude.

(ii) Frequency, $f=100 \mathrm{~Hz}$

(iii) Resistance, $R=820 \Omega$

(iv) Peak-to-peak voltage across resistance, $R=6.2 \mathrm{~V}$

From 0 to 5 msec , capacitor charge exponentially

$$ \begin{array}{ll} \therefore & V_c(\infty)=5 \mathrm{~V} \\ & V_c(0)=0 \mathrm{~V} \end{array} $$

(As capacitor is initially discharged)

Charging equation of first order RC circuit is given by,

$$ \therefore \quad \begin{aligned} & V_c(t)=V_c(\infty)+\left[V_c\left(0^{+}\right)-V_c(\infty)\right] e^{-t / \tau} \\ & V_c(t)=5+(0-5) e^{-t / \tau} \\ & V_c(t)=5\left(1-e^{-t / \tau}\right) \end{aligned} $$

From 5 msec to 10 msec , capacitor discharges through resistance.

$$ \text { As the voltage across resistance varies between } 3.1 \mathrm{~V} \text { to }-3.1 \mathrm{~V} \text { ( } 6.2 \mathrm{~V} \text { peak to peak) } $$

Voltage across resistance is given by,

$$ \begin{aligned} & V_R(t)=V_S-V_C(\tau)=5-\left[5\left(1-e^{-t / \tau}\right)\right]=5 e^{-t / \tau} \\ & V_R(t)=-3.1 \text { at } t=5 \mathrm{msec} \\ & -3.1=5 e^{\frac{-5 \times 10^{-3}}{820 \times C}} \\ & C=12.75 \mu \mathrm{~F} \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the value of $C$ is $\mathbf{1 2 . 7 5} \boldsymbol{\mu} \mathbf{F}$.
